 {"id":"bd-1on5","title":"Fix animation synchronization and flickering","description":"## Problem
Multiple animated elements running out of sync causing visual noise and flickering.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Largely Addressed - See bd-r9fy

The dashboard ALREADY has a global animation tick:
- `DashboardTickMsg` at 100ms intervals
- `m.animTick` counter in Model
- Passed to all animated components

### Remaining Issues
1. **components/progress.go** has orphaned independent ticks (50ms) - not used by dashboard
2. **Reduce motion** flag needs verification
3. **Animation smoothness** could be improved

## Dependencies
This bead depends on bd-r9fy which found the global tick already exists.

## Fix Implementation

### 1. Clean Orphaned Ticks
Remove unused independent ticks from components/progress.go

### 2. Verify Reduce Motion
```go
func TestReduceMotionDisablesAnimation(t *testing.T) {
    os.Setenv("NTM_REDUCE_MOTION", "1")
    defer os.Unsetenv("NTM_REDUCE_MOTION")

    result := styles.Shimmer("test", 42, "#FF0000", "#00FF00")
    // Should return static gradient, not animated
}
```

### 3. Consider Animation Smoothing
- Frame interpolation for smoother visual transitions
- Batch multiple updates into single render

## E2E Test (VHS)
```tape
# animation_sync_test.tape
Type "ntm dashboard --demo-data"
Sleep 5s
# All spinners should rotate in sync
Screenshot animation_sync.png
```

## Verification
- [ ] All spinners rotate in perfect sync
- [ ] NTM_REDUCE_MOTION=1 disables all animations
- [ ] No visual flickering during normal operation

## Priority
P3 - Most functionality already correct, cleanup task","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:10:03.332628451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:20:29.690896741Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:20:29.690385528Z","close_reason":"Animation sync confirmed working: global animTick at 100ms, NTM_REDUCE_MOTION support verified. Components use centralized dashboard tick.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1on5","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1on5","depends_on_id":"bd-r9fy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ovi8","title":"Task: Implement ensemble loader with user/project override support","description":"Implement user-defined ensemble configuration system with user/project overrides.

## Configuration File Format

### Locations
```
~/.config/ntm/ensembles.yaml
# or project-local
.ntm/ensembles.yaml
```

### Schema
```yaml
ensembles:
  my-security-review:
    display: "Security Review"
    description: "Security-focused code review ensemble"
    modes:
      - id: deductive         # A1
      - id: root-cause        # E2
      - id: worst-case        # G1
      - id: meta-cognitive    # L1
      - id: systems-thinking  # K1
    synthesis:
      strategy: adversarial
      synthesizer_mode: meta-cognitive
    budget:
      per_agent: 5000
      total: 35000
    allow_advanced: true
    agent_distribution:
      claude: 3
      codex: 2
    tags: [security, review]

  extended-architecture:
    extends: architecture-review
    modes:
      - id: formal-verification  # A3
    synthesis:
      strategy: deliberative

aliases:
  sec: my-security-review
  arch: architecture-review
```

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/config.go
type EnsembleConfigFile struct {
    Ensembles map[string]*UserEnsemble `yaml:"ensembles"`
    Aliases   map[string]string        `yaml:"aliases"`
}

type UserEnsemble struct {
    Display           string           `yaml:"display"`
    Description       string           `yaml:"description"`
    Extends           string           `yaml:"extends"`
    Modes             []ModeRef        `yaml:"modes"` // id or code
    Synthesis         SynthesisConfig  `yaml:"synthesis"`
    Budget            BudgetConfig     `yaml:"budget"`
    AllowAdvanced     bool             `yaml:"allow_advanced"`
    AgentDistribution map[string]int   `yaml:"agent_distribution"`
    Tags              []string         `yaml:"tags"`
}

func LoadEnsembleConfig() (*EnsembleConfigFile, error)
func (c *EnsembleConfigFile) GetEnsemble(name string) (*ResolvedEnsemble, error)
func (c *EnsembleConfigFile) ResolveAlias(name string) string
func MergeWithBuiltins(user *EnsembleConfigFile) *EnsembleConfigFile
```

## Validation
- Mode references resolve by id or code
- Strategy values valid (bd-2ztw9)
- Extends references existing ensemble
- No circular extends

## Dependencies
- Built-in ensembles (bd-cph20)
- Mode code support (bd-3806s)
- Strategy definitions (bd-2ztw9)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:41:24.472913894Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T02:57:27.211596389Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T02:57:27.211557906Z","close_reason":"Complete: ensemble_loader.go with multi-source precedence (embedded < user < project), EnsembleRegistry, GlobalEnsembleRegistry singleton, all tests passing","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ztw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-35e81","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-cph20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3eu7h","title":"Unit Tests: Robot Env Info","description":"Unit tests for Robot Env Info command.

### tmux Detection Tests

### Session Structure Tests

- Test pane index range parsing
- Test control vs agent pane identification

- Test shell type detection (zsh, bash, fish)
- Test oh-my-zsh detection
- Test config path discovery
- Test plugin enumeration

### Timing Tests
- Test default values returned
- Test config file override
- Test environment variable override
- Test priority order (config > env > default)

### Targeting Tests
- Test example generation correctness
- Test format string accuracy
- Test various session names (with special chars)

## Mocking Strategy

- Mock which/type commands
- Mock tmux list-* output
- Mock file system reads for config detection
- Mock environment variables

## Logging Requirements

Each test must log:
- Mock configuration at test start
- Command outputs being simulated
- Expected vs actual values
- Full response object on failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:30:00.562685465Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:25:22.665394075Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:25:22.665290850Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-1ws17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-28n0d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-2wkrv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-35xyt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eu7h","depends_on_id":"bd-cqnm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3exkg","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Probe","description":"E2E tests for Robot Probe with actual tmux sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Responsive Agent (keystroke_echo)
Spawn session, wait for agent initialization, execute probe, verify responsive=true

### Scenario 2: Unresponsive Pane (shell prompt)
Create session with just shell (no agent), execute probe, verify shell responds

### Scenario 3: Hung Process Simulation
Spawn session, make pane unresponsive with infinite loop, probe should detect unresponsive

### Scenario 4: Method Comparison
Test keystroke_echo vs interrupt_test methods, compare results

### Scenario 5: Custom Timeout
Test short (100ms) and long (10s) timeouts, verify behavior changes

### Scenario 6: Aggressive Mode Fallback
Test aggressive mode falls back to interrupt_test if keystroke_echo fails

### Scenario 7: Invalid Pane
Test with pane 999, verify PANE_NOT_FOUND error

### Scenario 8: Integration with Diagnose
Verify probe and diagnose results are consistent

## Logging Requirements
Each test logs: scenario name, setup params, probe params, result JSON, assertions, pass/fail, duration_ms

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 8 test scenarios implemented as bash scripts
2. Each scenario logs full JSON responses
3. Assertions verify specific JSON fields
4. Cleanup removes all test sessions
5. Tests are idempotent (can run multiple times)
6. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure
7. Total test suite runs in under 5 minutes","acceptance_criteria":"1. All 8 test scenarios pass
2. Test logs follow JSON format specification
3. Cleanup removes all test sessions
4. Tests are idempotent (can run multiple times)
5. Test duration under 5 minutes total
6. Each scenario logs setup/execute/verify/result
7. Exit code 0 on all pass, 1 on any failure
8. Test runner script executable: scripts/e2e_robot_probe.sh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:45.110817161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:44:02.389055346Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:44:02.388981058Z","close_reason":"E2E probe script already present and executable","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3exkg","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3exkg","depends_on_id":"bd-43oxm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ffb","title":"Add ntm send --dry-run to preview what would be sent to each agent without sending","description":"## Overview
Add --dry-run flag to ntm send to preview what would be sent to each agent without actually sending.

## Problem
Users want to verify prompt targeting before committing to sends, especially with complex targeting rules.

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --dry-run` shows exactly what would be sent without sending.

## Output Format
### Human Mode
```
Dry Run: ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt

Would send 5 prompts:
  1. cc_1: "Implement feature A" (from line 1)
  2. cc_2: "Implement feature B" (from line 5)
  3. cod_1: "Write tests for A" (from line 9)
  4. cod_2: "Write tests for B" (from line 13)
  5. gmi_1: "Review documentation" (from line 17)

Use without --dry-run to execute.
```

### Robot Mode
```json
{
  "dry_run": true,
  "would_send": [
    {"pane": 1, "agent": "cc_1", "prompt": "Implement...", "source": "line:1"},
    {"pane": 2, "agent": "cc_2", "prompt": "Implement...", "source": "line:5"}
  ],
  "total": 5
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Preview batch send
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --dry-run

# Preview with targeting
ntm send myproject --msg "Review code" --type cc --dry-run

# Preview assign
ntm assign myproject --dry-run
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No prompts actually sent in dry-run
- [ ] Output shows exact targeting
- [ ] Works with all send variants
- [ ] Robot mode JSON output
- [ ] Shows source (line number, bead ID)

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Dry-run flag prevents sending
- Unit test: Output format correctness
- E2E test: Dry-run matches actual send targeting","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3fg81","title":"External Project Integration Initiative","description":"Master EPIC for integrating 9 external Rust/Go projects into NTM to create a comprehensive multi-agent orchestration platform with safety, performance, observability, and knowledge management capabilities.

## Background & Strategic Vision

NTM (Named Tmux Manager) is a Go-based multi-agent orchestration platform that manages Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini agents in tmux panes. It provides ~60 robot mode JSON commands for AI agent automation, a TUI with Charmbracelet, command palette, and dashboard.

The 9 external projects represent mature, battle-tested tools that can dramatically enhance NTM's capabilities:

### Tier 1: Safety & Reliability (Immediate Priority)
- **DCG** (destructive_commit_guard): Blocks dangerous commands like rm -rf, git reset --hard
- **CAAM** (coding_agent_account_manager): OAuth vault with <100ms account switching for rate limit recovery
- **caut** (coding_agent_usage_tracker): Real-time quota/spend tracking across 16+ LLM providers

### Tier 2: Performance Optimizations
- **TOON** (toon_rust): Token-Optimized Object Notation - 40-60% token reduction vs JSON
- **RCH** (remote_compilation_helper): Offload compilation to remote workers, prevent CPU storms

### Tier 3: Observability Layer
- **rano**: Network observer for AI CLI processes - per-agent API call tracking
- **process_triage**: Bayesian classification of useful/abandoned/zombie processes

### Tier 4: Knowledge & Infrastructure
- **xf**: Hybrid search (BM25+semantic) for X/Twitter archives
- **rust_proxy**: Transparent iptables-based proxy with automatic failover

## Success Criteria
1. All Tier 1 integrations complete and tested
2. Robot mode commands added for each integration
3. Dashboard widgets for observability integrations
4. Comprehensive documentation in README.md
5. All integrations follow NTM's pure Go architecture (wrappers/FFI as needed)

## Architectural Principles
- NTM remains pure Go - integrate via CLI subprocess, IPC, or FFI
- Robot mode output must remain parseable (JSON or TOON)
- Each integration should be toggleable via config/flags
- Fail-safe: integrations must degrade gracefully if external tool unavailable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:41:23.616076852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T06:42:42.730744833Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":72,"issue_id":"bd-3fg81","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Roadmap & Rationale

### Why This Order?

**Tier 1 First (DCG → CAAM → caut)**:
- DCG provides immediate safety net with zero code changes needed in agents
- CAAM enables automatic recovery from rate limits (common pain point)
- caut gives visibility into spend before it becomes a problem

**Tier 2 Second (TOON → RCH)**:
- TOON reduces token costs across all robot mode interactions (compounds)
- RCH prevents CPU storms during parallel builds (common in multi-agent)

**Tier 3 Third (rano → process_triage)**:
- Both observability tools - see what's happening inside the swarm
- rano: network-level visibility (API calls, latency)
- process_triage: process-level health (stuck agents)

**Tier 4 Last (xf → rust_proxy)**:
- Nice-to-haves that enhance specific workflows
- xf: knowledge retrieval (useful but not critical path)
- rust_proxy: infrastructure layer (complex, can work without)

### Risk Mitigation

1. **FFI Complexity**: Rust integrations may need Go wrappers or subprocess calls
2. **Performance**: Subprocess overhead for real-time commands (mitigate with caching)
3. **Versioning**: External tools may update independently (pin versions, test CI)
4. **Config Sprawl**: Many new options (consolidate into [integrations] TOML section)

### Dependencies Between Projects

- TOON output requires caut to parse TOON (update caut if needed)
- rust_proxy can wrap CAAM OAuth calls for additional routing
- process_triage benefits from rano network data (correlation)
- DCG should whitelist RCH remote commands","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:42:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3fif","title":"Task: Implement audit logger with append-only storage","description":"## Overview
Create the core audit logging infrastructure with immutable, append-only storage.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/audit/logger.go:
   - AuditLogger struct
   - Log(entry AuditEntry) error
   - Close() error - flush and close log file

2. AuditEntry struct:
   - Timestamp: RFC3339 time
   - SessionID: NTM session identifier
   - EventType: command, spawn, send, response, error, state_change
   - Actor: user, agent, system
   - Target: what was affected
   - Payload: event-specific data (map[string]any)
   - Metadata: correlation_id, request_id, etc.
   - Checksum: SHA256 of entry (for tamper evidence)

3. Storage format:
   - JSONL (one JSON object per line)
   - File per session: ~/.local/share/ntm/audit/<session>-<date>.jsonl
   - Append-only (no modifications allowed)
   - File locking for concurrent access

4. Tamper evidence:
   - Each entry includes hash of previous entry
   - Enables verification of log integrity
   - Detect if entries were removed or modified

## Implementation Notes
- Buffer writes for performance (flush every N entries or M seconds)
- Handle disk full gracefully
- Compress old logs automatically

## Acceptance Criteria
- Entries written in correct format
- Append-only enforced
- Checksums validate correctly
- Concurrent writes work safely
- Unit tests for logging and validation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:59.785548538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T01:52:53.521364836Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T01:52:53.521322086Z","close_reason":"Completed audit logger implementation with tamper-evident hash chains, concurrent write safety, and comprehensive test coverage","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3fif","depends_on_id":"bd-3pbe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3jxy","title":"Fix list item deduplication in activity panel","description":"## Problem
The activity panel shows entries that APPEAR duplicated but may actually be different panes with similar names that become indistinguishable after truncation.

## Screenshot Evidence Analysis
Left panel shows approximately 17 rows all displaying "destructive_command_guar..." with different prefixes (★, blank).

**Key insight:** These are likely DIFFERENT panes:
- `destructive_command_guard_cc_1`
- `destructive_command_guard_cc_2`
- ...
- `destructive_command_guard_cc_17`

When truncated with end-ellipsis, they ALL become:
- `destructive_command_guar...` (INDISTINGUISHABLE)

## Root Cause: TWO Issues

### Issue A: Actual Duplicates (if present)
If the same entry truly appears multiple times, this is a data/render bug.

### Issue B: Truncation Making Different Items Look Identical (MORE LIKELY)
The TruncateRunes function cuts from the end, removing the distinguishing suffix.
**This is now fixed by bd-2qcn** which implements TruncateMiddle.

## Fix Implementation

### For Issue A: Deduplication
\`\`\`go
func (p *ActivityPanel) SetData(activities []Activity) {
    // Deduplicate by unique key
    seen := make(map[string]bool)
    unique := make([]Activity, 0, len(activities))
    for _, a := range activities {
        key := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", a.PaneID, a.Timestamp)
        if !seen[key] {
            seen[key] = true
            unique = append(unique, a)
        }
    }
    p.list.SetItems(unique)
}
\`\`\`

### For Issue B: Smart Truncation (PRIMARY FIX)
Use the new TruncateMiddle function from bd-2qcn:
\`\`\`go
func (p *ActivityPanel) renderItem(item Activity) string {
    // Use middle truncation to preserve distinguishing suffix
    name := layout.TruncateMiddle(item.Name, p.nameWidth)
    // Result: "destru…_cc_1", "destru…_cc_2" (DISTINGUISHABLE!)
    return name
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No TRUE duplicates (same pane ID appears once)
- [ ] Different panes with similar names are VISUALLY DISTINGUISHABLE
- [ ] Items are properly ordered (most recent first)
- [ ] Refresh doesn't cause duplication

## Unit Tests
\`\`\`go
func TestActivityPanel_NoDuplicates(t *testing.T) {
    panel := panels.NewActivityPanel()
    
    // Add activities with same ID (should dedupe)
    activities := []Activity{
        {ID: "pane_1", Title: "Task A"},
        {ID: "pane_1", Title: "Task A"}, // Duplicate
        {ID: "pane_2", Title: "Task B"},
    }
    
    panel.SetData(activities)
    
    // Should have 2 items, not 3
    if panel.ItemCount() != 2 {
        t.Errorf("expected 2 items after dedup, got %d", panel.ItemCount())
    }
}

func TestActivityPanel_SimilarNamesDistinguishable(t *testing.T) {
    panel := panels.NewActivityPanel()
    panel.SetSize(40, 20)
    
    // Add activities with similar names
    activities := []Activity{
        {ID: "1", Title: "destructive_command_guard_cc_1"},
        {ID: "2", Title: "destructive_command_guard_cc_2"},
        {ID: "16", Title: "destructive_command_guard_cc_16"},
    }
    
    panel.SetData(activities)
    view := panel.View()
    
    // Each truncated name should be unique in the view
    lines := strings.Split(view, "\n")
    seen := make(map[string]bool)
    for _, line := range lines {
        trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(line)
        if trimmed == "" || !strings.Contains(trimmed, "destru") {
            continue
        }
        if seen[trimmed] {
            t.Errorf("duplicate line in view: %q", trimmed)
        }
        seen[trimmed] = true
    }
}
\`\`\`

## E2E Tests
1. Launch dashboard with multiple similar-named panes - verify they're distinguishable
2. Spawn new pane - verify it appears once
3. Kill pane - verify it's removed
4. Rapid refresh - verify no accumulation

## Logging
\`\`\`go
log.Debug("activity panel rendering",
    "total_items", len(activities),
    "deduped_count", len(unique),
    "duplicates_removed", len(activities) - len(unique))

// Log if similar names detected
if hasSimilarNames(unique) {
    log.Debug("similar names detected, using middle truncation")
}
\`\`\`

## Dependencies
- **bd-2qcn** (TruncateMiddle) - PRIMARY dependency for making similar items distinguishable
- **bd-1xoe** (content height) - Ensures panel content doesn't overflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:08:18.819688166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:09:04.991439382Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:04:48.516792842Z","close_reason":"Fixed visually identical pane titles by implementing TruncatePaneTitle() in layout.go that preserves agent suffix (__cc_1, __gmi_2, etc.) when truncating. Panes with the same project prefix now remain distinguishable. Also cleaned up duplicate terminal package code.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jxy","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jxy","depends_on_id":"bd-2qcn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3jxy","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3k4sh","title":"Task: Implement synthesis prompt templates and strategy instructions","description":"# Task: Implement synthesis prompt templates and strategy instructions

Provide templated prompts for the synthesizer agent, with strategy-specific instructions and structured rendering of mode outputs.

## Component Overview
Templates structure how collected ModeOutput data is presented to the synthesizer agent.

## Base Template
```
# Ensemble Synthesis Task

You are the SYNTHESIZER for a reasoning ensemble. Multiple agents have analyzed the problem using distinct reasoning modes. Your task is to synthesize their outputs into a coherent, actionable analysis.

## Problem Statement
{{.Problem}}

## Synthesis Strategy: {{.Strategy}}
{{.StrategyInstructions}}

## Agent Outputs
{{range .Outputs}}
### {{.ModeName}} ({{.ModeID}}{{if .ModeCode}} / {{.ModeCode}}{{end}})
{{.Content}}
---
{{end}}

## Your Task
{{.SynthesisInstructions}}
```

## Strategy-Specific Instructions
- Align with strategy definitions in bd-2ztw9 (manual, adversarial, consensus, creative, analytical, deliberative, prioritized, dialectical, meta-reasoning, voting, argumentation-graph).
- Manual/voting/argumentation strategies may bypass the synthesizer agent; template should still exist for agent-based variants when requested.
- Each strategy should define explicit focus and required output sections.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/templates.go
type SynthesisTemplate struct {
    Name     string
    Strategy SynthesisStrategy
    Template *template.Template
}

func (t *SynthesisTemplate) Render(input *SynthesisInput) (string, error)
func LoadTemplates() map[SynthesisStrategy]*SynthesisTemplate
func GetTemplate(strategy SynthesisStrategy) (*SynthesisTemplate, error)
```

## File Location
internal/ensemble/templates.go

## Dependencies
- Strategy definitions (bd-2ztw9)
- Synthesis system feature (bd-2qwm8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Base template renders without error
- [ ] All strategies have templates
- [ ] Templates include all mode outputs in stable order
- [ ] Errors surfaced with helpful context","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:55.160470750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:37.462331499Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:57:37.462311050Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3k4sh","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3k4sh","depends_on_id":"bd-2ztw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kb1","title":"Task: Implement RateLimitTracker","description":"## Purpose
Track rate limit events and learn optimal spawn/send timing.

## RateLimitTracker Struct
type RateLimitTracker struct {
    mu      sync.RWMutex
    history map[string][]RateLimitEvent // provider -> events
    delays  map[string]time.Duration    // provider -> current optimal delay
}

type RateLimitEvent struct {
    Time     time.Time
    Provider string
    Action   string // 'spawn' or 'send'
}

## Key Methods
func (t *RateLimitTracker) RecordRateLimit(provider string)
func (t *RateLimitTracker) GetOptimalDelay(provider string) time.Duration
func (t *RateLimitTracker) RecordSuccess(provider string)

## Adaptive Learning
- On rate limit: increase delay by 50%
- On 10 consecutive successes: decrease delay by 10%
- Never go below minimum (anthropic: 5s, openai: 3s)

## Default Delays
anthropic: 15s
openai: 10s
google: 8s

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tracks rate limit events
- Adjusts delays adaptively
- Persists learned delays
- Thread-safe

## Technical Notes
Store in .ntm/rate_limits.json","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
